svg.text: Trie — Prefix Tree for String Sets
Each node represents a prefix; edges are characters.
A boolean flag marks "end of a word".
Operations on string of length L:
insert: O(L); search exact: O(L); search prefix: O(L)
delete: O(L); space O(N * L) worst case (one node per char in the worst input)
Shared-prefix structure ⟹ much smaller than N * L when prefixes overlap.
• Fixed array (size 26 for lowercase) — fastest, sparse for non-alphabetic data
• Hash map of char to node — flexible alphabet, slight overhead
• Compressed trie (radix / Patricia) — collapses single-child chains
• Ternary search tree — three children per node, smaller for sparse alphabets
For Leetcode-style alphabetic input: int[26] children + boolean isEnd is the default.
Trie wins when many strings share prefixes and queries are prefix-based.
